name: anaconda-mcp
description: Manage conda environments and packages from MCP clients
long_description:
Anaconda MCP brings conda environment management to MCP clients. It uses the bundled `ana` CLI to install and run the Anaconda MCP server, so users do not need to pre-create an `anaconda-mcp` conda environment.

**Requirements:**
- A supported operating system: macOS Apple Silicon, Linux x86_64/aarch64, or Windows x86_64.
- Network access on first launch so `ana` can install its managed Anaconda MCP runtime.
- An Anaconda API key configured during MCPB installation, or existing credentials from `ana login` / `anaconda login`.
- Accepted Anaconda MCP Beta Terms before tool calls will succeed. The install-time setting can record acceptance for this bundle; otherwise run `anaconda mcp terms accept` outside the MCP server.

**Scope:**
- This MCPB bundle installs and runs the Anaconda MCP server through bundled `ana`.
- It does not install Miniconda, Anaconda Distribution, or a user-facing `conda` command, and it does not add `conda` to the user's `PATH`.
- Environments created through Anaconda MCP can be managed through the MCP tools. To activate or inspect them from a terminal, notebook, or other local tooling, users need a separate conda installation configured for that workflow.

**What you can do:**
- List all conda environments
- Create new conda environments with specific packages
- Delete/remove conda environments
- Install packages into environments
- Delete/remove packages from environments

tools.name: create_environment
tools.description: Create a new conda environment with optional packages and channels

tools.name: delete_environment
tools.description: Delete a conda environment by name or prefix path

tools.name: remove_environment
tools.description: Remove a conda environment by name or prefix path (alias for delete)

tools.name: install_packages
tools.description: Install packages into a conda environment

tools.name: delete_packages
tools.description: Delete packages from a conda environment

tools.name: remove_packages
tools.description: Remove packages from a conda environment (alias for delete)

anaconda_api_key.title: Anaconda API key
anaconda_api_key.description: Optional API key from your Anaconda account settings. Leave empty to use existing credentials from `ana login` or `anaconda login`; MCP startup does not run interactive login.

accept_beta_terms.title: Accept Anaconda MCP Beta Terms
accept_beta_terms.description: Set true only if you accept the Anaconda MCP Beta Terms at https://www.anaconda.com/legal/terms/mcpbeta. Otherwise accept terms outside the server with `anaconda mcp terms accept`.
